preamble: The transformation from a voiced palatal lateral approximant (/ʎ/) to a voiced palatal plosive (/ɟ/) is a phonetic process known as fortition. This shift involves an increase in the degree of stricture, changing an approximant, which has a relatively open articulation, into a plosive, which requires a complete closure of the vocal tract followed by a release.
phoneticEffects: Fortition, increase in stricture, approximant to plosive shift

examples.note: The historical voiced palatal lateral approximant /ʎ/, traditionally represented by the digraph 'll' in Spanish, has undergone fortition to a voiced palatal plosive /ɟ/ in certain varieties of Latin American Spanish. This is part of a broader phonological drift known as yeísmo or zheísmo, which can also lead to affricated or fricated pronunciations.

sources: Workflows in historical linguistics | Freelance reconstruction - WordPress.com. (2017, July 14).
